title: Evolution Education Notebook Preview
description: A SciSiteForge notebook-mode example for beginner-facing concept study, guided routes, multilingual visibility, and source-aware learning surfaces.

hero.title: A beginner-friendly evolution notebook can be a real public study surface.
hero.lede: This example shows how SciSiteForge can publish concept pages, guided routes, multilingual queue visibility, and source-aware learning materials without turning the site into a dynamic application.

feature_cards.title: New to evolution?
feature_cards.body: Use a short beginner route that explains what evolution is, why populations matter, and what to read next.

feature_cards.title: Working with a concept cluster
feature_cards.body: Core concepts like allele frequency change, drift, selection, and common descent can be grouped into a guided first ring.

section_cards.title: Population Thinking
section_cards.body: A threshold idea can be promoted across several concept pages so readers stop treating evolution as change in isolated individuals.

section_cards.title: Source status guidance
section_cards.body: Notebook pages can stay honest about citation and translation maturity while still helping a reader decide what is reliable for learning now.

bibliography_entries.title: doclift
bibliography_entries.body: Recovered source documents and normalized content can feed the reader-facing Notebook surface.

bibliography_entries.title: GroundRecall
bibliography_entries.body: Grounded concepts, claims, and provenance provide the review memory behind notebook pages.

bibliography_entries.title: Didactopus
bibliography_entries.body: Learning paths and mentorship-oriented sequencing can consume the same Notebook artifacts.

notebook_lede: A public-facing notebook can orient beginners, connect related concepts, and expose machine-readable artifacts without making them the primary user experience.

notebooks.title: Core Evolution Concepts
notebooks.summary: A first-ring notebook groups beginner-facing concepts into a path that moves from population change to larger historical patterns.
goals: Give new readers a clear first fifteen minutes
Keep concept pages readable on their own
Show how source status and translation status affect trust without overwhelming the learner

apps.title: Allele Tracker
apps.description: A population-change sandbox linked to the first concepts in the route

roadmap.title: Add a real beginner route
roadmap.body: Use the landing page and notebook front door to make the first fifteen minutes obvious for a new reader.

roadmap.title: Promote threshold concepts
roadmap.body: Concepts like population thinking should be visible across multiple pages and may deserve their own page.

roadmap.title: Keep translations visible before they are complete
roadmap.body: Planned languages should remain visible in the site shell, with queue and fallback status showing what work remains.

roadmap.title: Treat regression checks as part of the build
roadmap.body: Notebook mode should fail fast if required public pages or translation-status artifacts disappear.
